name: Muhammad Musa
tagline: M.S. Computer Sciences at UW–Madison. I build AI that reaches the people the field usually skips — safer language models, healthcare records for hospitals that never had them, and NLP for languages the internet forgot.
availability: Graduating 2026 · open to AI/ML engineering & applied-research roles

highlights: Graduate coursework in Machine Learning, Language-Based Security, Computer Vision, Privacy & Security in Data Science, Big Data Systems, Foundation Models, and AI Safety & Differential Privacy
Straight-A first year: Machine Learning (CS 760), Language-Based Security (CS 839), Advanced Seminar (CS 900)
Head Graduate TA for CS 320 and Graduate TA for CS 220 — data-science programming for 1,600+ students across both roles
Dean's Honor List in every single academic semester
Top-15 Merit Scholarship Award, sophomore and junior year
Senior thesis on ransomware provenance — published at ACM CSET '23
Coursework: Algorithms, Databases, Data Science, Linear Algebra, Statistics, AI, ML, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Data Mining, NLP, Network Security, Signal Processing

experience.summary: Founded and personally built Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic ecosystem app — an AI-assisted, FHIR/HL7-compliant EHR platform for a healthcare system still running on paper.
bullets: Founded Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic ecosystem app, unifying 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs onto one FHIR/HL7-compliant digital platform
Built the product end-to-end in Flutter/Dart, PostgreSQL, and Supabase — cross-platform (iOS/Android/Web) with role-based auth, ACID-compliant storage, and real-time sync
Shipped EHR modules: patient onboarding, appointment booking, lab integrations, e-prescriptions, QR-based health IDs, and encrypted report uploads with audit trails
Implemented AI/ML pipelines (Python, TensorFlow Lite, and Claude/Gemini/GPT) for doctor–patient conversation summarization, lab anomaly detection, and diagnostic support
Built REST + gRPC microservices on PostgreSQL, Supabase Realtime, and AWS S3; tuned queries with GIN indices to scale across millions of records

Directed the MVP rollout across 10 clinics and 5 labs within 4 months — while personally writing over 70% of the initial codebase and leading a cross-functional team of engineers, clinicians, and partners

experience.role: Head Graduate Teaching Assistant — CS 320: Data Science Programming II
experience.summary: Lead a 20-TA teaching staff for 500+ students, and quietly automate the parts of teaching that don't need a human.
bullets: Automated the end-to-end grading pipeline for 500+ students with Python, the GitLab API, and the Canvas API — cutting grading turnaround by 90%
Built a MOSS-based plagiarism-detection workflow enforcing academic integrity across 1,000+ submissions
Lead a team of 20 TAs — coordinating assignment logistics, grading consistency, and student support for a 500–600-student course
Prototype AI-powered, real-time student assistance using Ollama and local LLM frameworks

experience.role: Graduate Teaching Assistant — CS 220: Data Science Programming I
experience.summary: Guided 1,100 students through their first real data-science programming in Python.
bullets: Led lab sessions and one-on-one guidance for 1,100 students in Python, data manipulation, analysis, and visualization
Supported grading and built project and quiz material for an inclusive, large-scale intro course

experience.summary: Put ML and computer vision into a 60-year-old engineering firm's design workflow.
bullets: Built AI-powered architectural design tools with Python, TensorFlow, and the AutoCAD API for automated 3D modeling and design optimization
Developed a predictive-maintenance model to anticipate structural issues — improving safety by 15% in simulated stress tests
Integrated LLMs and computer vision into BIM workflows for natural-language project queries and real-time anomaly detection
Engineered automated drafting/modeling pipelines (Python, AutoCAD API, CI/CD), cutting manual design effort by 40%
Built scalable Python backends (Flask, FastAPI) for design-data management and API integration

experience.role: Head Teaching Assistant — CS 331: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
experience.summary: Wrote every programming assignment, from scratch, for 200 students learning AI.
bullets: Authored comprehensive programming assignments for 200 students spanning neural networks, Bayesian networks, reinforcement learning, and CSP solvers
Mentored students through the hard parts of AI theory and implementation

experience.summary: Shipped on-chain educational credentials and payment-ready web apps as an undergrad.
bullets: Built an NFT-based certification system on the STACKS blockchain using Clarity smart contracts, improving credential integrity and verification
Deployed the minting app with ReactJS and Next.js to issue educational certificates as on-chain NFTs
Built responsive, performance-optimized web apps for a CPaaS platform and integrated Stripe payments

projects.name: LLM Jailbreaking & Safety-Alignment Research
projects.description: Extended the Tree-of-Attacks-with-Pruning (TAP) jailbreaking framework with Dr. Somesh Jha to study how fine-tuning quietly degrades the safety alignment of open-source LLMs.
highlights: Boosted jailbreak success from 4% to 68% with fewer queries than GPT-4
Built a multi-evaluator scoring framework to cut false positives
Quantified how fine-tuning strips safety guardrails off open models

projects.name: Low-Resource Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP
projects.description: Curated the first large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi corpus and fine-tuned a transformer for topic classification — a new NLP benchmark for a language tens of millions speak but the internet barely reads.
highlights: 44,000-point dataset (2,500 labelled) via translation, transliteration, and augmentation
92.9% topic-classification accuracy — a new benchmark for Shahmukhi NLP
5× reduction in labeled-data needs through active learning

projects.name: WUMI Health — AI-assisted EHR platform
projects.description: Founded and built Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic ecosystem app, unifying hospitals, clinics, and labs onto one FHIR/HL7-compliant platform with generative-AI clinical support.
highlights: Consolidated 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs into one platform
MVP live across 10 clinics and 5 labs within 4 months
Personally wrote 70%+ of the initial codebase

projects.name: resume-gauntlet — adversarial multi-agent résumé engine
projects.description: An open-source Claude Code plugin that turns a folder of real work into interview-defensible résumé bullets, then attacks them. Its core is a 7-verifier adversarial gauntlet — ATS simulator, skeptical hiring-manager, plausibility auditor, AI-fingerprint detector and more — that every bullet must survive. Writer and verifier models are forced to differ at runtime, so nothing grades its own homework. (This site's honesty-first AI twin runs on the same ethos.)
highlights: 32 skills + 23 subagents (writer vs. verifier roles), writer≠verifier enforced at runtime
7-verifier adversarial gauntlet gates every claim before it ships
374+ automated tests, Stop-hook guardrails, MIT-licensed

projects.name: Voice Agent — $0 fully-local outreach caller
projects.description: An AI outreach pipeline that discovers local businesses, drafts a grounded pitch, and can hold a real phone conversation — running entirely on free, local models. A deterministic call-state machine owns the conversation flow so the LLM can never go off-script or invent commitments.
highlights: End-to-end voice loop: VAD → speech-to-text → local LLM → text-to-speech
Deterministic FSM keeps the call on-rails; a separate verifier model blocks unsourceable claims
688 tests across 101 files — built solo on a $0 budget

projects.name: Ransomware Provenance Analysis
projects.description: Undergraduate senior thesis with SRI International (Dr. Ashish Gehani) and Dr. Fareed Zaffar (LUMS): an automated pipeline for large-scale ransomware behavioral analysis via provenance graphs. Published at ACM CSET '23.
highlights: Published at ACM CSET '23 as first co-author
Automated detonation → real-time monitoring → provenance-graph generation at scale
Built a large-scale provenance dataset from thousands of malware samples

projects.name: House Boundary Detection from Satellite Imagery
projects.description: Applied semantic segmentation to detect house boundaries in low-resolution satellite imagery of Pakistani cities, under Dr. Murtaza Taj (LUMS).
highlights: High-precision boundary detection on the Multi-temporal Urban Development SpaceNet dataset
Results secured funding for follow-on research

projects.name: Urban Sentiment & Extremism Detection
projects.description: A context-aware framework for extremist-content detection through urban sentiment analysis on Twitter, under Dr. Momin Ayub. Poster at IEEE/ACM ASONAM '22.
highlights: Poster published at IEEE/ACM ASONAM '22
Combined scraping, topic modelling, and geospatial visualization for policy-relevant insight

projects.name: Adversarial Attack on a Sentiment Classifier
projects.description: Built a CNN/LSTM sentiment classifier and demonstrated its fragility to minimal adversarial token substitution.
highlights: 90% baseline accuracy on 50,000 processed reviews
Cut model accuracy by 50% by replacing just 3 tokens per input

projects.name: Real-Time Multi-Camera Mask Detector
projects.description: A real-time mask-compliance detection system fusing three camera angles into a single top-view projection with homography.
highlights: Real-time detection of mask-SOP violations
Fused 3 camera angles into one top-view projection for robust coverage

projects.name: TUBITAK TEKNOFEST — Fixed-Wing UAV
projects.description: Led the electronics and systems sub-team designing a fixed-wing UAV for an international robotics competition.
highlights: Led a team of 7
Selected among the top 20 teams globally

research.title: Ransomware Provenance Analysis
research.venue: ACM CSET '23 — Workshop on Cyber Security Experimentation and Test
research.description: Automated end-to-end pipeline for ransomware behavioral detection using provenance graphs (SPADE), with Dr. Ashish Gehani (SRI International) and Dr. Fareed Zaffar (LUMS). First co-author.

research.title: Urban Sentiment Analysis for Extremism Detection
research.venue: ASONAM '22 — IEEE/ACM Int'l Conf. on Advances in Social Networks Analysis and Mining (poster)
research.description: Context-aware framework for extremist-content detection via urban sentiment analysis on Twitter, advised by Dr. Momin Ayub.

research.title: Active Learning for Low-Resource Language Fine-Tuning (Shahmukhi Punjabi)
research.description: First large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP corpus with active-learning-driven fine-tuning of XLM-RoBERTa, reaching 92.9% topic-classification accuracy.

awards: Dean's Honor List, LUMS — every academic semester
Top-15 Merit Scholarship Award, LUMS — sophomore & junior year
TUBITAK TEKNOFEST UAV — top 20 teams globally

ai_for_humanity.why: I'm drawn to the parts of AI that touch people who are easy to overlook. The through-line in my work isn't a slogan — it's what I actually chose to spend my nights on. An EHR platform for hospitals in Pakistan that were still running on paper. The first real NLP benchmark for Shahmukhi Punjabi, a language tens of millions speak and the internet barely reads. Safety research on how fine-tuning quietly strips the guardrails off open models. To me, "AI for humanity" doesn't mean working on the biggest, flashiest model in the room. It means asking, every single time: does this actually help someone who was previously unheard? That question is why I got into this — and it's the filter I want my next role to pass.
themes: Healthcare access & EHR interoperability for underserved regions
Public & structural safety (extremism, ransomware, predictive maintenance)
evidence: Founded WUMI Health to bring unified, AI-assisted electronic health records to a fragmented healthcare system serving millions — using generative AI for clinical summarization and diagnostic support
Built the first large-scale NLP corpus and benchmark for Shahmukhi Punjabi, explicitly to close an equity gap in language technology
Researched LLM jailbreaking and safety-alignment degradation under Dr. Somesh Jha — studying how to keep increasingly capable, accessible AI systems safe
Published ransomware-detection research (ACM CSET '23) aimed at protecting people and organizations from cyberattacks
Built an extremism-detection framework (ASONAM '22) applying NLP to public-safety-relevant sentiment analysis
Developed a predictive-maintenance model at SAUDCONSULT explicitly to improve structural safety, not just efficiency
Deliberately took graduate coursework in AI Safety & Differential Privacy alongside core ML — sustained academic commitment to responsible AI

journey_milestones.title: Born in Pakistan
journey_milestones.subtitle: Where the story starts
journey_milestones.description: Born September 9, 2001. Family roots in Pakistan shaped a career that keeps circling back to one idea: bringing technology to the people and places that usually get skipped.

journey_milestones.title: B.Sc. Computer Science at LUMS
journey_milestones.subtitle: Dean's Honor List, every semester
journey_milestones.description: Studied CS at Lahore University of Management Sciences — 3.9/4.0 GPA, Dean's Honor List every single semester. Led student organizations, taught AI and blockchain as a TA, and built his first research relationships with faculty.

journey_milestones.title: First ventures: UAVs, blockchain, hackathons
journey_milestones.subtitle: Leading before graduating
journey_milestones.description: Led a 7-person team to a top-20 global finish at TUBITAK TEKNOFEST with a fixed-wing UAV, placed 3rd in a STACKS blockchain hackathon, and shipped an NFT-based certification system on-chain for KASB Securities.

journey_milestones.title: Research takes root
journey_milestones.subtitle: Satellite imagery, social media, and ransomware
journey_milestones.description: Built a house-boundary detection model on satellite imagery of Pakistani cities, published a sentiment-analysis poster at ASONAM '22 on extremism detection, and completed a senior thesis on ransomware provenance with SRI International — published at ACM CSET '23.

journey_milestones.title: Moves to Madison for grad school
journey_milestones.subtitle: M.S. Computer Sciences, UW–Madison
journey_milestones.description: Began graduate study in Computer Sciences at UW–Madison, posting a 4.0 across Machine Learning, Language-Based Security, and an advanced seminar in the first semester — and immediately started teaching, guiding 1,100 students through CS 220.

journey_milestones.title: AI safety research & teaching at scale
journey_milestones.subtitle: Breaking LLMs to make them safer
journey_milestones.description: Extended Tree-of-Attacks-with-Pruning jailbreak research with Dr. Somesh Jha — pushing jailbreak success from 4% to 68% to expose safety-alignment decay. Interned in Riyadh building AI architectural tools at SAUDCONSULT, and became Head TA for CS 320, automating grading for 500+ students and cutting turnaround 90%.

journey_milestones.title: Closing the language gap
journey_milestones.subtitle: First large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP benchmark
journey_milestones.description: Curated the first large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi corpus and fine-tuned XLM-RoBERTa to 92.9% accuracy, using active learning to cut labeling cost 5× — a new benchmark for a language NLP had largely ignored.

journey_milestones.title: Founds WUMI Health
journey_milestones.subtitle: Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic platform
journey_milestones.description: Turned years of AI-for-good research interest into a real product — founding and personally building an AI-assisted EHR platform unifying 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs. Wrote 70%+ of the code himself and shipped an MVP live in 10 clinics within 4 months.

journey_milestones.title: What's next
journey_milestones.subtitle: Research, engineering, and impact converge
journey_milestones.description: Finishing the M.S. at UW–Madison and looking for an AI/ML role where the work matters — carrying forward one consistent thread across research, teaching, and building: using AI to serve real people.
